size_bucket: flash-base (moe, ~500B-class by parameter count inference; 43 layers, 256 routed experts, 6 per token, 1 shared expert, 4096 hidden, FP8 weights)

run_context.phase: new-model Phase 1.2 (arch-check before recipe + env build)

attrs.architecture_extractor_gap: DeepSeek uses n_routed_experts / n_shared_experts (not num_local_experts / num_experts). extract_attrs.py heuristic does not recognize this MoE naming convention — augmented manually below.
architecture_requires_modeling_file_insp: gated_router_with_rmsnorm (DeepSeek V3/V4 routers commonly use noaux_tc with scoring_func sqrtsoftplus; whether the gate applies an internal RMSNorm on its input is not readable from config.json)
rmsnorm_gated (Mamba-style RMSNormGated; no evidence in config but cannot be fully ruled out without inspecting modeling_deepseek_v4.py)
attrs.config_note: No recipe supplied (Phase 1.2 runs before recipe creation). All entries with config triggers are evaluated as insufficient-info and treated as skipped for firing but must be re-checked once a recipe exists.
attrs.deps_note: vllm submodule not present under 3rdparty/ — branch deepseek-v4-support has no vllm checkout. VLM-path ledger entries evaluated with vllm=unknown.
novel_config_fields_not_in_ledger_vocabu: hc_eps / hc_mult / hc_sinkhorn_iters (hash-clustered routing? Sinkhorn-iteration signal is unique to DeepSeek V4)
index_head_dim / index_n_heads / index_topk (indexer / sparse-attention layer, unfamiliar shape)
compress_ratios (43-entry per-layer list mixing 0, 4, 128 — suggests per-layer KV-compression or layer-specific sparse attention)
q_lora_rank / o_lora_rank (MLA-style low-rank Q and O projections; V3 only had q/kv lora, this adds o_lora)
num_nextn_predict_layers=1 (MTP head; DeepSeek V3 had this too — in NeMo-RL it needs to be dropped or frozen during bring-up)
quantization_config.fmt=e4m3 with scale_fmt=ue8m0 and block size 128x128 (FP8 block-quantized weights on disk — requires dequant or native FP8 support during load)
sliding_window=128 (new vs. V3 which was pure full-attention; suggests hybrid attention per layer)

inspect.question: Does the DeepSeek V4 decoder layer send a pre-normed residual (post_attention_layernorm / pre_feedforward_layernorm) into a MoE.forward() whose internal gate also applies its own RMSNorm on the same input tensor?
inspect.hint: Read modeling_deepseek_v4.py (or HF remote code cache under the model dir). Trace decoder forward -> moe(x) -> gate(x). If gate.norm is an nn.Module that runs before scoring, check whether x is the raw residual or the pre-normed residual. A double-norm on the gate input mis-routes experts while still training, producing elevated gen_kl_error (~0.1 class) without a hard error — exactly the Gemma4 26B-A4B pattern.
inspect.why_it_applies: DeepSeek V4 is MoE with 256 routed experts, scoring_func=sqrtsoftplus, topk_method=noaux_tc. The scoring_func suggests a custom router; the ledger entry fires on the shape 'gated_router_with_rmsnorm' which is not extractable from config.json. Must be answered before trusting KL metrics.

inspect.question: Does the automodel implementation of DeepseekV4 expose experts via nn.ModuleDict (or nn.ModuleList wrapped by ModuleDict), and is there a registered ParallelizationStrategy that casts expert DTensors with fully_shard_by_dtype?
inspect.hint: grep automodel (3rdparty/Automodel-workspace/Automodel) for DeepseekV4 model class and its ParallelizationStrategy registration. The Gemma4 failure mode is: automodel 2013a4dd added ModuleDict handling but Gemma4's MoE uses a different EP mesh than the DP MixedPrecisionPolicy mesh, so expert weights stay float32. DeepSeek V4 MoE on a 256-expert EP mesh is structurally similar and may inherit the same trap.
inspect.why_it_applies: automodel HEAD (92635e74) is past 2013a4dd (the commit that introduced the ModuleDict handling). MoE + ep_sharded_experts are present. The only unmet piece is the ModuleDict-vs-custom-strategy check.

inspect.question: Does DeepSeek V4's attention or MoE path invoke vLLM's RMSNormGated (as opposed to plain RMSNorm)?
inspect.hint: RMSNormGated is typically bound to Mamba or gated-linear-unit blocks. DeepSeek V4 has swiglu_limit=10.0 and no mamba_hybrid signal, but sliding_window=128 + compress_ratios suggests a custom attention variant. Quickly inspect modeling_deepseek_v4.py for RMSNormGated; if absent, dismiss this entry.
inspect.why_it_applies: Cannot be ruled out from config.json alone; vLLM submodule is not checked out on this branch so the current sha is unknown, and NeMo-RL runs with enforce_eager=true by default.

info.caller_note: Still worth remembering: DeepSeek V4 has num_nextn_predict_layers=1 (MTP head) which may be frozen / unused during RL and could trigger a similar 'missing optim state for frozen param' failure on ckpt resume even though the ledger trigger does not explicitly match. Re-evaluate once the recipe decides how to handle the MTP head.

info.reason_not_fired: family qwen3.5 only; DeepSeek-V4 is not in scope

entries_deferred_until_recipe: transformers-tie-weights-meta-tensor (needs world_size, though tied_embeddings already disqualifies)
skipped_insufficient_info.note: Re-run arch-check with recipe=<yaml> after Phase 3.

action_required: Resolve the three INSPECT items by reading the DeepSeek V4 modeling file before Phase 3 recipe creation:
1) gemma4-moe-double-norm-gate — trace decoder -> moe.forward -> gate to verify no double-norm on gate input.
2) gemma4-moe-expert-dtype-fsdp2-moduledict — check whether automodel DeepseekV4 path uses ModuleDict for experts and whether fully_shard_by_dtype is registered.
3) vllm-rmsnormgated-missing-activation-enforce-eager — confirm RMSNormGated is not on the DeepSeek V4 code path.
After recipe YAML exists, re-run: python .claude/skills/arch-check/extract_attrs.py --model ... --recipe <yaml> ...
novel_risks_not_in_ledger: FP8 block-quantized checkpoint (e4m3 weights, ue8m0 scales, 128x128 block). NeMo-RL's automodel path has historically loaded bf16/fp16; block-FP8 load + upcast or native FP8 kernel availability is an open question and should be validated in env-profile / smoke-test.

Per-layer compress_ratios array mixing {0, 4, 128} over 43 layers suggests hybrid KV compression (some layers full, some 4x, some 128x). Any parallelism (TP/PP) plan must respect per-layer differences.

Dual RoPE bases (rope_theta=10000 main, compress_rope_theta=160000 for compressed path) plus YaRN factor=16 with original_max_position_embeddings=65536 (effective 1048576) — verify that automodel + vLLM handle the dual-theta config correctly; only one rope_theta is typically plumbed through.
sqrtsoftplus scoring function with noaux_tc topk — this is not a standard softmax-topk MoE router. Expert routing diff vs HF reference may need a standalone-logit-diff check in smoke-test.
num_hash_layers=3 and hc_* (hash-clustered / Sinkhorn) parameters are entirely new vocabulary. Consider filing a new ledger entry once the pattern is understood.

num_nextn_predict_layers=1 (MTP) — DeepSeek V3 also had this; typically dropped at load time for NeMo-RL. Confirm automodel drops or isolates the MTP head so its params do not participate in the optimizer and break ckpt resume.
